Calculation.Text: "Enter a description of the usage of the list"

StyledText.Data: Name your list group with an understandable name. It makes it easier when you connect your Page item to the list when authoring.

Calculation.Text: FAT ModifierValue @ Title::Modifier id = FAT Title::<Field Missing>

SetProperty ( "numberOfChildrenField"; GetFieldName ( FAT List::Number of Lists in Group)) & SetProperty ( "sortField"; GetFieldName ( FAT List of Lists @ List::SortIndex )) & SetProperty ( "action"; "up")

SetProperty ( "numberOfChildrenField"; GetFieldName ( FAT List::Number of Lists in Group )) & SetProperty ( "sortField"; GetFieldName ( FAT List of Lists @ List::SortIndex )) & SetProperty ( "action"; "down")

StyledText.Data: Number of items: <<FAT List @ List of Lists::Number of List items>>

ScriptReference.name: Add List to List of Lists

StyledText.Data: The first list you add in the list group defines the type of list.

Calculation.Text: "View all blocks in this Title that can use this IO type. "

StyledText.Data: Number of lists in list group: <<FAT List::Number of Lists in Group>>

Calculation.Text: IsEmpty(FAT List::PrimaryKey) Or IsEmpty(FAT List::List template id) Or IsEmpty ( FAT List::Usage type )

StyledText.Data: A list can be used for filtering page items on a page. When choosing Usage = "Tag filter" the following happens: 1. Created items will also be available as Tags. 2. When authoring, author can tag page items using the tag function. Don't forget the multiple choice question In order to filter content based on a user choice, you must first add a Multiple choice building block in your flow. The Multiple Choice question needs to be connected to this list. Then it will use the list as Tags. Please not, you can only have save the tag filter choice in one IO in the Title for each List and Tag category. For the user A user launch the package and check appropriate options in the Multiple choice question. From now, the package will only display page items that are either not tagged or tagged with the same tag as the user checked in the Multiple choice question. For all other uses - select Usage = "Default"

CharacterStyle.Text: Don't forget the multiple choice question

ParagraphStyle.Text: 1. Created items will also be available as Tags. 2. When authoring, author can tag page items using the tag function.
